children.name: Very High Loyalty Enrollment Target — Apr16v7
children.description: Top-tier propensity for loyalty program enrollment above 0.8. Tests propensityForLoyaltyProgram at a higher threshold.

children.name: Fully Profiled Color and Advisor — Apr16v7
children.description: Has both color preference and advisor ID set. Tests IS NOT NULL on two different field groups.

children.name: Size Preference M or L — Apr16v7
children.description: Profiles who prefer size M or L clothing. Tests prefSize with IN list.

children.name: High Average Order Value — Apr16v7
children.description: Profiles with average order value above 150. Tests recentOrderAmountAvg numeric field.

children.name: Offer Engager 2 Plus — Apr16v7
children.description: Profiles who redeemed more than 2 offers in the last 6 months. Tests offersRedeemedLast6Months field.

children.name: Active Buyer Gold Tier — Apr16v7
children.description: Gold members with more than 3 YTD orders. Tests ordersYTD numeric + tier equality together.

children.name: Mid Upgrade Propensity — Apr16v7
children.description: Loyalty upgrade propensity between 0.5 and 0.7 — the middle bucket. Tests range with two comparisons.

children.name: Points Elite Gold Platinum — Apr16v7
children.description: 5000+ loyalty points AND gold or platinum tier. Tests numeric comparison + IN list on two fields.

children.name: Gold Loyalty Tier — Apr16v7
children.description: Profiles with gold loyalty tier. Simplest possible equality check. Streaming so it evaluates quickly.

answer: Based on the provided evidence, there is no data or records regarding live journeys or a schema named 'Journey schema with Journey Fields for Journey Orchestration'. The retrieved segments (audiences) from the database do not contain any references to journeys in their definitions or names, so it is not possible to determine which properties from that schema are used.
